analysis.summary: This file implements the V-trace algorithm, an off-policy correction method for actor-critic reinforcement learning, adapted for PyTorch. It computes importance-weighted value targets and policy gradient advantages by processing behavior and target policy log-probabilities, rewards, discounts, and value estimates over a trajectory. The implementation includes both standard tensor operations and a GPT-specific variant that explicitly handles 3D shapes for sequence, batch, and parallel dimensions. It serves as a mathematical utility to stabilize off-policy updates in distributed RL setups like IMPALA. Readers should understand how clipped importance weights prevent variance explosion during off-policy training.
insights: V-trace stabilizes off-policy learning by clipping importance weights using configurable thresholds for value targets and policy gradients separately.
The algorithm computes value targets and advantages via a reverse-time scan, accumulating discounted rewards and bootstrapped value estimates.
The GPT-specific variant explicitly manages 3D tensor dimensions (time, batch, parallel/agents) to support sequence-based or multi-agent RL workloads.
